findings.claim: In plain-R use, a placeholder's "usable outside Shiny" promise is VALUE-equivalence only (`ppVar(x)` is the identity, returns `x`); it does NOT cover dplyr auto-naming, which derives column names from `rlang::as_label()` of the source text at quote-time (before `ppVar` runs), so `group_by(ppVar(continent))` yields a column literally named `ppVar(continent)`.
findings.detail: Cannot be fixed in `ppVar`'s body (the leak is syntactic, and dplyr ignores value-level `names()`). Workarounds are call-site only: explicit `name = ppVar(col)`, or strip after with `rename_with(~ sub("^pp[A-Za-z]+\\((.*)\\)$","\\1",.x))`. A product fix would be an exported name-strip helper, not a smarter `ppVar`.
findings.verification: builtins.R:397 `ppVar <- function(x=NULL,...) x` (identity); dplyr derives column names from rlang::as_label(expr) at quote-time, before ppVar runs, so the call text leaks. Confirmed rename_with strip + explicit name= are the only fixes; value-level names() ignored by dplyr.

findings.claim: A 0-argument call is pruned to `ptr_missing` only when its name is in `remove_set` = `default_drop_when_empty()` ∪ user `safe_to_remove`; `default_drop_when_empty()` is library data-verbs ONLY (ggplot2/dplyr/tidyr/tibble/pillar/purrr/stringr/forcats/lubridate/hms), so base predicates like `is.na` are NOT pruned by default — you opt them in via the public `safe_to_remove` arg.
findings.detail: This is why "we prune empty calls" is true but didn't help `is.na()`: empty-call pruning is name-gated, and `is.na ∉` the default set. `safe_to_remove = "is.na"` makes `is.na()` prune → `!` propagates → `filter` drops.
findings.verification: prune.R:101-105 prunes a 0-arg call only when its name is in remove_set; utils.R:211 default_drop_when_empty is library data-verbs only. Confirmed safe_to_remove='is.na' flips is.na() to pruned.

findings.claim: Because the public boundary functions capture the formula with `rlang::enexpr()`, any `!!`/`!!!` injection operator on a placeholder is processed AT CAPTURE — the placeholder call is evaluated (its embellish identity returns the bareword value) and spliced to literals, so the placeholder node never reaches substitution/UI and silently gets no widget.
findings.detail: e.g. `between(year, !!!ppRange(c(1957,2007)))` captures as `between(year, 1957, 2007)` — `ppRange` is gone. Symptom: "custom placeholder UI not rendered." Rule: never apply `!!`/`!!!` to a placeholder inside a ggpaintr formula; it must survive capture as a bare call node. (Same reason a standalone `expr()`/`enexpr()` of such a formula errors "could not find function ppRange" when the fn isn't defined yet.)
findings.verification: app.R:141 captures via rlang::enexpr(formula); enexpr runs !!/!!! at capture-time, evaluating the placeholder (identity) and splicing literals. Reproduced this session: capture of `!!!ppRange(c(1957,2007))` yields `between(year,1957,2007)` with the ppRange node gone.

findings.claim: A placeholder's POSITIONAL argument is the widget-seeding channel — it flows `node$default` → `state$spec_seed[[id]]` → `build_ui`'s `selected` — so it cannot be repurposed to carry non-seed data (e.g. a column symbol); pass auxiliary params via NAMED args (`node$named_args`), which is never written to spec_seed.
findings.detail: For a CUSTOM keyword the spec_seed write hits no type-coercion branch, so a positional column symbol passes through unchanged into `selected`, and `build_ui`'s `as.numeric(selected)` errors on a symbol (suppressWarnings does NOT catch errors) → control never renders. Correct pattern for "placeholder needs a column arg": `parse_named_args = list(col = ptr_arg_symbol())`, read `node$named_args$col` in resolve_expr; positional stays empty so seeding has nothing to coerce.
findings.source: R/paintr-translate.R:964 (default <- entry$default_arg(positional[[1]])); R/paintr-server.R:630-665 (spec_seed write); R/paintr-build-ui.R:47-64 (selected from spec_seed)
findings.verification: translate.R:964 `default <- entry$default_arg(positional[[1L]])`; the default feeds state$spec_seed -> build_ui_for.ptr_ph_value (build-ui.R:47, seeds `selected` from spec_seed). Confirmed a positional column symbol flows into `selected` and build_ui's as.numeric(symbol) errors; named_args is the non-seed channel.

findings.claim: The prune policy (`safe_to_remove`, `is_standalone`) MUST be threaded identically into BOTH prune sites — render (`ptr_complete_expr_safe`) and upstream-data (`ptr_resolve_upstream`) — or consumer column-scoping silently diverges from the rendered plot; the render path threaded `safe_to_remove`, the upstream path did not (bug fixed 2026-06-02, commit 12d236f).
findings.detail: Symptom: `safe_to_remove="is.na"` fixed the plot render (filter dropped) but left the upstream eval choking on `is.na()` → NULL → empty downstream pickers. `ptr_run_formula` (headless) had the same divergence (threaded to render, not to `ptr_headless_upstream_cols`). Fix is behavior-preserving on the default path (empty `safe_to_remove` → identical pruning); `is_standalone` left NULL at every caller, mirroring the render path. Two `ptr_prune()` call sites total, no `prune_walk()` bypass — the surface is fully enumerable.
findings.source: R/paintr-resolve.R:ptr_resolve_upstream; R/paintr-safe.R:34 (ptr_complete_expr_safe); R/paintr-server.R (4 resolve_upstream sites); R/paintr-headless.R:ptr_run_formula
findings.verification: Render path safe.R:36 threads safe_to_remove into ptr_prune; upstream resolve.R:41 now forwards safe_to_remove+is_standalone (fixed commit 12d236f); server.R threads state$safe_to_remove at 2083/2118/2890/3259; headless ptr_run_formula/ptr_headless_upstream_cols threaded. Full suite green (PASS 3638/FAIL 0).

findings.claim: Missingness propagates UP a call tree only through `pruneable_operator_names` (symbolic operators only: arith, comparison, `&`/`|`, `:`, `%in%`, `~`, `!`); for any NAMED function call a missing argument is instead DROPPED, and the call becomes missing only if it ends 0-arg AND its name is in `remove_set`.
findings.detail: So `is.na(ppVar(sex))` with ppVar missing collapses to `is.na()` (a NON-missing empty call), which keeps the enclosing `!` and `filter` alive (renders the broken `filter(!is.na())`). Trims that route the placeholder through an operator (`ppVar(sex)=="male"`) or make it a verb's DIRECT arg (`filter(ppNotNA(sex))`) prune cleanly; wrapping it in a non-operator predicate does not.
findings.verification: prune.R:89-96 operator branch returns ptr_missing if any arg missing; :97-106 else-branch drops missing args and only escalates a 0-arg call. utils.R:468 pruneable_operator_names is symbolic operators only (no named fns). Confirmed is.na(missing)->is.na() kept via prune probe.

findings.claim: `ptr_arg_symbol()` validates that the arg is a symbol but RETURNS `rlang::as_string(el)` — a character string, not a symbol — so a `resolve_expr` that wants to splice it as a bare column reference must convert back with `rlang::sym()`, else it injects a string literal (`"year" >= 1957`, the wrong comparison).
findings.verification: default-args.R:274 `return(rlang::as_string(el))` inside ptr_arg_symbol's element validator — returns a string. Confirmed resolve produced `"year" >= 1957` until rlang::sym() was applied.

findings.claim: `ptr_resolve_upstream()` is the per-position path that substitutes+prunes+EVALS a data subtree to a data frame purely to populate downstream consumers' (var pickers') column choices; an eval failure returns NULL (its `tryCatch`), and a NULL upstream starves every downstream picker (renders to nothing).
findings.detail: Distinct from the plot-render path (`ptr_complete_expr_safe`, R/paintr-safe.R). Called at server.R 2076 (shared host resolution), 2110 (per-position consumer cols), 2881 (renderUI picker), 3249 (panel source replacement), plus headless `ptr_headless_upstream_cols`. A broken upstream stage (e.g. `filter(!is.na())`) erroring here is why deselecting a consumer can blank UNRELATED downstream pickers.
findings.verification: resolve.R:9 substitutes+prunes+evals a subtree to a data frame (NULL on eval error via tryCatch); server.R per-position consumer-scope callers at 2076/2111/2883/3252.

findings.claim: Placeholder substitution is strictly one-slot→one-node: `substitute_walk.ptr_call` replaces each argument position with exactly one substituted node, and `ptr_ph_value` wraps `resolve_expr`'s return in a single `ptr_literal` — there is no splice, so a placeholder cannot fan out into multiple sibling arguments of its parent call.
findings.detail: Consequence: `filter(between(year, ppRange()))` can never work if ppRange is meant to fill both `left` and `right` — resolve_expr returning `c(lo,hi)` is ONE object in ONE slot → `between(year, c(lo,hi))` (right missing → error). Workarounds: make the single node resolve to the whole expression (e.g. `filter(ppRange(col))` → `col>=lo & col<=hi`), or consume the vector via a helper `in_range(year, ppRange())`. Splicing is exactly what `!!!` would do, and that's consumed at capture (see [[enexpr-consumes-injection-operators]]).
findings.verification: substitute.R:59 substitute_walk.ptr_call rewrites each arg position to exactly one node; :128 wraps resolve_expr's return in a single ptr_literal. No splice path exists.
